alternatives: Expose one public target per feature
Rely on convention to prevent implementation imports
consequences: Cross-feature consumers depend on stable contracts
Composition roots retain narrowly scoped construction access
Implementation declarations remain internal by default
context: Feature modules need a stable cross-feature surface without exposing construction and implementation details.
decision: Separate public IO contract targets from implementation targets; cross-feature consumers import IO only, while declared composition roots may access explicitly registered composition entries.
migration: Split mixed targets, move shared contracts to IO, internalize implementation declarations, and expose only required composition entries.
title: Contract versus implementation targets
verification_impact: The final joined AI consistency review confirms target boundaries, visibility, and reciprocal Rule/Profile/ADR references.
